title: hepetis heterophylla var. exscapa
intro: Hepetis heterophylla var. exscapa, also known as Japanese peppermint, is a perennial herb native to East Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains menthone and limonene, offering analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Japanese peppermint include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, such as nausea, indigestion, and flatulence, as well as its use in treating headaches and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Hepetis heterophylla var. exscapa are used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
